title: lora_config return checked but lora_send may have different error semantics
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:312-317,513-518

lora_config() and lora_send() returns are checked, but the error handling assumes all negative returns are errors. Zephyr LoRa API may return specific codes (-EBUSY, -EINVAL, etc.) that could benefit from specific handling.

What goes wrong: Generic error logging (-EBUSY vs -EINVAL) provides less debugging information. A busy radio might be retried, while an invalid config requires code fix.

Low priority since current error propagation is correct, but specific error handling could improve debuggability.
close_reason: Added comment documenting that lora_send() follows same error semantics as lora_config(): returns 0 on success, negative errno on failure, with common error codes listed
